# Figure description: скв_152.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/drill_holes_data/скв_152.jpg`

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite borehole/well log (геологический разрез скважины / каротажная диаграмма)** — a standard Soviet-era composite well log combining lithological/stratigraphic columns with geophysical wire-line log curves. It appears to be a full-length borehole log strip chart, typical of Soviet mineral exploration drilling documentation (буровой журнал with геофизический каротаж).

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a **single deep borehole** (скважина), though the specific borehole number is **[illegible]** at this scan resolution. The log spans a considerable depth range — based on the length of the strip and typical Soviet 1:200,000 survey drilling, likely on the order of **several hundred to possibly 1,000+ metres**. The depth scale runs continuously from top (shallow) to bottom (deep) along the left margin. The figure belongs to the regional survey of **map sheets M-42 and/or N-42** in northern Kazakhstan (Smolianova et al., 1983–1984).

The document is formatted as a **tall, narrow composite log strip** (approximately 3:1 height-to-width ratio), consistent with Soviet standard бланк каротажа (logging form).

---

## Key Content

The figure contains several vertical columns arranged side by side, from left to right, as follows:

1. **Leftmost columns (lithological description zone):**
   - A **lithological symbol column** showing rock type patterns (dots, hatching, brick patterns, stippling) — indicating alternating lithologies such as sandstones, clays/mudstones, siltstones, and possibly carbonates or crystalline basement rocks. The patterns change frequently in the upper portion and become more uniform toward the bottom.
   - Narrow columns adjacent to the lithology column likely contain **depth markers** (in metres), **core recovery data (%)**, and **formation/stratigraphic unit designations** — though most text here is **[illegible]** at this resolution.

2. **Central geophysical curve section (the dominant visual feature):**
   - Two or more **continuous wiggle-trace curves** running the full length of the log. These are almost certainly:
     - **КС (кажущееся сопротивление / apparent resistivity)** curves — showing characteristic high-amplitude spikes and deflections
     - **ПС (потенциал самополяризации / spontaneous potential / SP)** curve — smoother, lower-amplitude deflections
   - The curves show **high-amplitude anomalies** (large deflections) in the **upper-to-middle depth range**, with a particularly prominent **large spike/anomaly** visible roughly one-quarter to one-third of the way down the log. This may indicate a resistive unit (e.g., tight sandstone, carbonate, or mineralized zone).
   - In the **lower half** of the log, the curves become more subdued and regular, suggesting more homogeneous lithology (possibly basement or massive sedimentary sequence).
   - A distinct **break or change in character** is visible roughly two-thirds of the way down, possibly representing a **lithological boundary or unconformity**.

3. **Right-side data table columns:**
   - Multiple narrow columnar tables on the **right side** of the strip contain **numerical data** — likely formation tops, sample depths, laboratory analysis results (e.g., grain size, porosity, mineral content), or geophysical parameter values. Most entries are **[illegible]** but the column structure with rows corresponding to depth intervals is clear.
   - There appear to be approximately **8–12 data columns** on the right side with headers at the top.

4. **A boxed inset/legend** is visible in the **lower-left quadrant** of the figure — likely containing the **legend (условные обозначения)** for lithological symbols used in the log column.

5. **A second boxed section** appears in the **middle-right area** — possibly a summary table of key intervals or formation thicknesses.

- **Boxed legend (lower left):** Contains small text with lithological symbol descriptions — **[illegible]** individually but structurally consistent with standard Soviet условные обозначения.

## Research Value

This composite borehole log provides a **continuous record of subsurface lithology and geophysical properties** (primarily resistivity and SP) from surface to total depth, serving as ground-truth for the regional seismic and potential-field surveys conducted under Report 36572. A geologist would use this to **correlate subsurface formations, identify potential reservoir or mineralized intervals** (particularly at the prominent resistivity anomaly zones), and establish the depth to basement or key stratigraphic boundaries across the M-42/N-42 survey area. For an AI research assistant, this log is critical for **calibrating geophysical interpretations** — linking surface geophysical anomalies to specific rock units at depth and assessing mineral or hydrocarbon potential of identified intervals.
